{{Quote|The slivilith doesn't have much of a mind, as we know it, but I was able to pick up some rather muddy telepathic images. Apparently, the monster drifted through space in a sort of hibernating state for quite a number of years—before being caught in Arbra's gravitational pull and drawn planetward. Once within our atmosphere, it gained mobility—and a goal. After floating so long in the constant chill of space. It sought warmth. Unfortunately, the warmest spot on the planet, due to the geothermal power rods, was our cave!|[[Plif/Legends|Plif]]|<ref name="marvel55" />}}
The '''slivilith''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of large, [[Color/Legends|green]], non-[[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] creatures originally created by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] as a long-range intelligence gathering device thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before their eventual [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Fearsome predators with little mind of their own, slivilith could travel immense distances across the vacuum of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in a sort of hibernation state before awakening on re-entry into a new [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], after which it immediately sought heat. Although they frequently appeared in popular entertainment, slivilith were often considered myths by the galaxy at large. Only four encounters with slivilith had been confirmed by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], including a battle between a slivilith and a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] operatives on [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Slivilith-MSW55.jpg|thumb|left|250px|A slivilith takes wing.]]
Massive, [[Color/Legends|green]]<ref name="marvel55" /> [[Bat/Legends|bat]]-like creatures,<ref name="comicscan">{{GalaxyCite|3|ComicScan|ComicScan: Marvelously Silly Aliens}}</ref> slivilith were the size of a small [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], flying on large wings spread out from its bulbous body. Its round head was highlighted by a gaping maw and a pair of wide-set red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and it had a series of tentacles that trailed from the end of its body. Slivilith were also remarkably hardy creatures, able to survive for indefinite periods in the cold vacuum of [[Realspace/Legends|space]].<ref name="aa" />

Their wings, while ungainly looking, generated enough power to allow slivilith to break free of a planet's [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] pull and escape into the void, propelling itself with the force required to travel between worlds. The slivilith could enter into a hibernation state where it survived on its own internal gases and stored nutrients for centuries, only awakening due to the heat caused by re-entry into a new [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. The fact that slivilith were only ever seen alone and spent much of their lives hibernating between worlds led many scientists to believe they were [[Gender/Legends|hermaphrodites]].<ref name="aa" />

==Behavior and intelligence==
Slivilith possessed little intelligence of their own,<ref name="comicscan" /> and little was known of their life cycle, diet, or method of reproduction. However, what was certain was that they were vicious and fearsome predators, hunting and capturing prey by ensnaring it with their tentacles and pulling it into its maw. Slivilith were reputed to consume everything on a [[Planet/Legends|world]] before moving onto the next one, awakening when entering a new planet and immediately finding a new place to lair. Slivilith prioritized finding and maintaining its safe haven over anything else, including finding nourishment—unusual for most creatures that existed in the vacuum of space, which mostly preferred the cold of the void, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]-created slivilith were compelled by their archaic genetic programming to seek warmth and heat immediately upon leaving hibernation.<ref name="aa" />

==History==
[[File:Ballinspace.png|thumb|right|250px|A slivilith travels through space en route to Arbra.]]
The slivilith were creations of the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] race known as the Yuuzhan Vong, skilled [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineers]] who created the beasts as an early experiment in developing a living long-range intelligence gathering device. Although the Yuuzhan Vong would not invade [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] until thousands of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, the slivilith had started to appear in the galaxy as far back as the [[Old Sith Wars]],<ref name="aa" /> used as scouts by the vanguard force called the [[Praetorite Vong]].<ref name="intrigue">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], slivilith were widely considered to be myths by the galaxy at large, as there had been only four verified encounters with slivilith over the previous four thousand years. However, they became popular fixtures in entertainment, featuring frequently in [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[holo-serial]]s during the final years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and serving as the villains in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-era holo-serial ''[[The Voyages of the VSD Protector]]''.<ref name="aa" />

Although the true story of the slivilith's origins eluded researchers, their existence and origins was a hot topic of debate for centuries, with the wider scientific consensus agreeing that they had not evolved naturally. Multiple schools of thinking developed on the slivilith's origins, with one camp believing that [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] [[Geneticist/Legends|geneticists]] had created the slivilith, while still others crediting them as [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] creations of the ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. During the Yuuzhan Vong War some researchers had begun to posit that the extragalactic aliens were in fact responsible, but most scholars rejected the theory.<ref name="aa" />

==Slivilith in the galaxy==
{{Quote|If this doesn't work, a thousand blasters won't save us! Just keep firing—until that flying slug gets close enough... for... this! Now, Chewie! Now!|Leia Organa, as her Rebels battle a slivilith on Arbra|<ref name="marvel55" />}}
Slivilith were extremely rare in the wider galaxy, leading most to think of them as little more than a tale. However, by the era of the Galactic Civil War there had been four confirmed encounters with slivilith: one shortly after the [[Freedon Nadd Uprising]] almost four thousand years before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], two during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]],<ref name="aa" /> and one by a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] on the planet [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. In that instance, a slivilith had nested itself inside a cave important to the native [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]], forcing the diminutive [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] away from the [[power rod]]s they relied on for sustenance. When a Rebel scouting party led by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] arrived on Arbra to evaluate it as a potential site for a new Alliance [[Haven Base|base]], they allied with the Hoojibs to fight the slivilith. The Rebels managed to defeat the fearsome beast through the strength of the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], who grabbed hold of the slivilith's tentacles and impaled it on the cavern's power rods.<ref name="marvel55" />

==Behind the scenes==
The slivilith first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 55|''Star Wars'' (1977) 55]], written by [[David Michelinie]] and released in [[1981]].<ref name="marvel55" /> The creature's origins and backstory were later greatly expanded upon in the [[2001]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] sourcebook ''[[Alien Anthology]]'', which established it as being a Yuuzhan Vong creation.<ref name="aa" />
